agent_scope.summary: Contracts for package-validator behavior around direct smoke execution, AppBlock and CodeBlock boundary validation, and previewer registration, routing, manifests, assets, and session stamping.

contracts.title: Smoke tests execute Block.run directly and propagate failures
contracts.contract: A package validator smoke test for runtime transport must instantiate only Block subclasses, pass optional params through BlockConfig params, call instance.run(inputs, config), and return that output mapping. Non-Block classes are strict TypeError failures, and any exception raised by run() propagates to the caller. This helper observes a block boundary; it does not by itself prove worker serialization, lineage, or storage durability.

code_evidence.notes: The harness directly executes the block runtime boundary.

code_evidence.pattern: raise TypeError(f"{cls!r} is not a subclass of Block. Cannot run smoke test.")

tests.notes: Smoke test returns the block output mapping.
Runtime exceptions propagate.
Non-Block targets are rejected.

contracts.notes: ADR-020 governs the broader runtime transport model, but no ADR found in this pass defines BlockTestHarness.smoke_test itself.
contracts.validator_implication: Block package validation can use smoke_test as a development-time executable check and should treat raised exceptions as blocking smoke failures.

contracts.title: Collection transport remains runtime-specific at external boundaries
contracts.contract: Package validation must not reduce data transport to a single metadata-only rule. AppBlock unwraps Collection inputs for tool-specific file exchange and rewraps collected artifacts as Collections; CodeBlock v2 materializes declared file-exchange ports, writes a manifest, invokes a backend, and reconstructs declared outputs. A validator may inspect declarations statically, but executable transport proof requires a smoke or runtime test.

code_evidence.notes: AppBlock unpacks Collection inputs before file exchange.

adr_evidence.pattern: ### 7.2 CodeBlock Boundary
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-020 describes CodeBlock collection boundary behavior.

adr_evidence.pattern: ### 7.3 AppBlock And External Tool Boundary
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-020 delegates AppBlock exchange shape to the external tool block.

tests.notes: CodeBlock file-exchange port tests cover boundary artifacts.

contracts.notes: Static package validation can identify likely boundary declarations, but current implementation makes actual data movement a runtime concern.
contracts.validator_implication: Emit a warning when only static declarations were inspected; block only when an executable smoke/runtime check or strict boundary validator fails.

contracts.title: CodeBlock v2 persisted config validation is strict for concrete CodeBlock specs
contracts.contract: CodeBlock package validation must use the concrete CodeBlock v2 config contract: strip runtime-only keys, reject legacy inline/function/runner fields, require project-local paths, require supported script extensions, validate port direction/name/exchange_folder/data_type/capability lookup, and return CodeBlockValidationDiagnostic entries whose default severity is error.

code_evidence.notes: Port declarations participate in strict validation.

code_evidence.pattern: Inline CodeBlock configs are not valid CodeBlock v2 configs.
code_evidence.notes: Legacy inline configs become migration errors.

code_evidence.pattern: CodeBlock v2 does not call SciStudio entry functions; scripts must use file exchange.
code_evidence.notes: Entry-function script configs are rejected by the v2 config model.

adr_evidence.pattern: CodeBlock v2 is narrowed by ADR-041 toward project-local script file exchange
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-005 records the current CodeBlock v2 narrowing.

adr_evidence.pattern: CodeBlock exchange and workflow-validator tests cover the current v2 script
adr_evidence.notes: ADR verification references strict v2 diagnostics.

tests.notes: Workflow validator renders CodeBlock v2 errors.
Legacy inline configs are reported as errors.

contracts.notes: The current code treats these as diagnostics objects internally, then workflow validation renders error-severity diagnostics as save-time errors.
contracts.validator_implication: A package validator should block concrete CodeBlock declarations that produce error-severity CodeBlockValidationDiagnostic entries.

contracts.title: Workflow validation enforces AppBlock output ambiguity and CodeBlock strictness
contracts.contract: When a registry is provided, validate_workflow enforces boundary checks for variadic cardinality, AppBlock duplicate output-port extensions, and concrete CodeBlock v2 declarations. Duplicate AppBlock extensions and CodeBlock error diagnostics are strict errors. Unknown block types are returned as warning strings and skip deeper type and CodeBlock validation.

code_evidence.notes: AppBlock duplicate extension check is a workflow boundary validator.

code_evidence.pattern: diagnostic.render(node_id=node.id) for diagnostic in diagnostics if diagnostic.severity == "error"
code_evidence.notes: Only error-severity CodeBlock diagnostics become workflow errors.
Unknown registry specs are diagnostic warnings, not deep validation failures.

adr_evidence.pattern: `AppBlock` is the external-software block category.
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-006 identifies AppBlock as the external tool boundary.

adr_evidence.pattern: CodeBlock is the block category for user-provided code.
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-005 identifies CodeBlock as the user-code boundary.

tests.notes: AppBlock duplicate extension workflow checks.
Validator reads frontend-style params.output_ports.
CodeBlock validation applies only to concrete CodeBlock specs.
Unknown block registry entries remain warnings.

contracts.notes: Current implementation returns all messages as strings; consumers distinguish warnings by message text, while CodeBlock diagnostic severity is explicit before rendering.
contracts.validator_implication: Block package validation should block duplicate AppBlock output extensions and concrete CodeBlock error diagnostics, but report unknown or unavailable registry entries as diagnostics unless the package contract requires full registry resolution.

contracts.title: Concrete AppBlock and CodeBlock subclasses inherit boundary contracts with drift-sensitive checks
contracts.contract: Concrete package subclasses of AppBlock and CodeBlock should be inspected as boundary participants. AppBlock subclasses that override run() must preserve extension-based output routing when output_ports are configured. CodeBlock subclasses delegating to CodeBlock.run must provide v2 file-exchange config and must not rely on legacy language, mode, or entry_function fields unless the package validator classifies that as a migration failure.

code_evidence.notes: Implementation currently patches a field that CodeBlock v2 rejects.

adr_evidence.pattern: ADR-041 rejects inline execution in CodeBlock v2
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-005 records the v2 rejection of framework-shaped CodeBlock configs.

adr_evidence.pattern: AppBlock tests verify file-exchange preparation and output reconstruction.
adr_evidence.notes: ADR-006 aligns with AppBlock file-exchange subclass behavior.

tests.notes: Existing AccuCorR run test bypasses actual CodeBlock.run validation.
Skeleton test confirms CodeBlock subclass identity.

contracts.notes: Drift is code-first: AccuCorR patches entry_function/language/mode before delegating, while CodeBlock v2 validation rejects entry_function and legacy runner fields. The current AccuCorR run test monkeypatches CodeBlock.run, so it does not exercise the real v2 delegate.
contracts.validator_implication: Package validation should at least warn on CodeBlock subclasses that synthesize legacy fields; it should block when the concrete runtime config is passed through validate_codeblock_config and produces error diagnostics.

contracts.title: Previewer entry points return PreviewerSpec lists and registry failures are diagnostic
contracts.contract: A package previewer surface is declared with the scistudio.previewers entry-point group. The entry point resolves to a zero-argument factory returning a list or tuple of PreviewerSpec objects. PreviewerRegistry loads core specs, entry-point specs, and optional monorepo package specs; duplicate IDs, empty IDs, broken factories, non-list returns, and non-PreviewerSpec items are recorded as diagnostics or skipped instead of crashing the registry.

code_evidence.notes: Registry uses the package entry-point group.

code_evidence.pattern: previewer factory '{source}' returned non-PreviewerSpec item; skipping

adr_evidence.pattern: A previewer may be supplied by core, an installed package
package previewers from `scistudio.previewers` entry points

tests.notes: Duplicate IDs are diagnostic.
Bad factory return type is diagnostic.
Imaging package entry point is pinned.

contracts.notes: Current registry behavior is diagnostic-first. A future package validator may choose to escalate diagnostics for publish-time validation, but that is stricter than the runtime registry.
contracts.validator_implication: Report registry diagnostics and malformed factories; block only when the package-validation profile explicitly requires a clean previewer registry.

contracts.title: PreviewerSpec and FrontendManifest define the public previewer declaration shape
contracts.contract: PreviewerSpec declares previewer_id, owner_kind, owner_name, target_type, supports_collection, priority, capabilities, backend_provider, optional frontend_manifest, and api_version. FrontendManifest declares previewer_id, module_url, export_name, css, version, api_version, and backend-only asset_root. The public wire shape must omit asset_root; value validity is enforced by registry and asset validators rather than dataclass construction.

code_evidence.pattern: Wire shape sent to the frontend. ``asset_root`` is intentionally omitted.

adr_evidence.pattern: | `previewer_id` | Stable globally unique ID
The manifest must name the previewer ID

tests.notes: Backend-only asset_root is not serialized.
Session envelope manifest wire shape omits asset_root.

contracts.notes: The dataclasses are frozen declaration models, not full validators. Empty IDs and remote URLs are handled by registry/assets.
contracts.validator_implication: Validate the declaration shape, then delegate value-level checks to PreviewerRegistry and validate_manifest/resolve_asset.

contracts.title: Frontend manifest validation separates blocking invalidity from diagnostics
contracts.contract: validate_manifest returns ManifestValidation. A missing manifest, missing previewer_id, missing module_url, remote module_url, remote css URL, or missing export_name makes the manifest invalid. An api_version mismatch and missing version are diagnostics; api_version mismatch sets api_version_ok false but does not by itself flip valid to false. resolve_asset is stricter: it raises MissingBundleError for missing asset_root, remote asset URLs, path traversal outside asset_root, disallowed suffixes, or absent files, and returns ServedAsset only for path-confined allowed files.

code_evidence.pattern: def resolve_asset(manifest: FrontendManifest, relative_path: str) -> ServedAsset:

code_evidence.notes: API version mismatch is tracked separately from validity.
Path confinement failure is a strict MissingBundleError.

adr_evidence.pattern: Package and project frontend modules are loaded only through backend-validated
FR-024: Package-owned frontend assets must be wheel-packaged

tests.notes: Positive manifest and asset resolution coverage.
Current imaging manifest has matching API version.

contracts.notes: Current code distinguishes invalid manifests from diagnostic-compatible API-version drift. resolve_asset is exception-based and should be treated as strict.
contracts.validator_implication: Block remote, path-escaping, missing-root, missing-file, and invalid required-field cases; warn on API-version mismatch unless the validation profile escalates compatibility drift.

contracts.title: PreviewRouter selection is deterministic and ambiguous matches are strict errors
contracts.contract: PreviewRouter must resolve a PreviewTarget by collection-aware type specificity and owner tier: project exact, package exact, project parent, package parent, core collection fallback, core base fallback, then unknown/error. Highest priority wins within a bucket. Unresolved priority ties raise RoutingAmbiguityError, and no-match cases raise UnknownTargetError. A project default may resolve a tie for its target type.

code_evidence.notes: Ambiguous candidates are strict typed errors.
No previewer match is a strict typed error.
Project defaults can resolve priority ties.

adr_evidence.pattern: Resolution order is:
FR-004: Within the same precedence tier and type specificity
adr_evidence.notes: Spec defines priority and ambiguity behavior.

tests.notes: Ambiguous tie raises RoutingAmbiguityError.
Collection targets do not fall through to item-only previewers.
Project default resolves ties.

contracts.notes: PreviewSessionManager converts PreviewError from routing into error envelopes for one-shot rendering, but the router itself is strict exception-based.
contracts.validator_implication: Block package configurations that would produce unresolved routing ambiguity; report UnknownTargetError only when the validation scenario expects a previewer to exist.

contracts.title: Core and imaging package PreviewerSpec declarations establish fallback and package ownership
contracts.contract: Core previewer specs are registered unconditionally for DataFrame, Array, Series, Text, Artifact, CompositeData, Collection, PlotArtifact, and DataObject fallback targets. The imaging package get_previewers factory returns Image and Label package-owned specs with positive priority, backend providers, capabilities, and same-origin frontend manifests so package exact matches can beat core fallbacks while core remains available when imaging is absent.

code_evidence.notes: Core fallback specs are concrete PreviewerSpec declarations.
Imaging package spec declares a frontend manifest.

adr_evidence.pattern: FR-012: Core must provide fallback previewers for DataFrame
FR-025: `scistudio-blocks-imaging` must register package previewers for

tests.notes: Core fallback registry coverage.
Imaging package PreviewerSpec coverage.
Core fallback remains available without imaging.

contracts.notes: This row covers non-required concrete PreviewerSpec declarations from the inventory so package-validator design does not focus only on registry classes.
contracts.validator_implication: Use info-level reporting for discovered fallback/package spec inventories, escalating only for malformed IDs, missing providers where required, or routing ambiguity.

contracts.title: Session rendering stamps manifests and turns provider failures into error envelopes
contracts.contract: PreviewSessionManager resolves a spec, invokes its backend provider, converts PreviewError and unexpected provider exceptions into PreviewEnvelope error responses, and stamps the resolved spec frontend_manifest onto the envelope when the provider did not set one. A provider-set frontend_manifest has precedence over the spec default.

code_evidence.notes: Typed provider/routing errors become error envelopes.
Unexpected provider exceptions are also converted.

code_evidence.pattern: if envelope.frontend_manifest is None and spec.frontend_manifest is not None:

code_evidence.notes: Missing-spec helper is a concrete PreviewerSpec declaration from the inventory.

adr_evidence.pattern: The session manager stamps the resolved previewer's `frontend_manifest`
FR-029: The implementation must add deterministic diagnostic payloads

tests.notes: Spec manifest is stamped when provider omits one.
Provider manifest precedence is tested.

contracts.notes: Session behavior makes previewer provider failures non-fatal to the API surface even when the underlying router/provider raises typed or unexpected exceptions.
contracts.validator_implication: Validate manifest ownership at the spec/session seam and classify provider exceptions as typed error-envelope behavior rather than process crashes.
